question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxIdleConnections] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Pool.MaxIdleConnections larger than Pool.MaxOpenConnections to create a configuration inconsistency that causes the DB driver to behave unexpectedly, starving 30%+ of node-related queries of connections?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.Online] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Online mode toggle to cause the server to initialize an SDK client without a mirror network (offline mode workaround path), resulting in the SDK having no knowledge of 30%+ of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.ReadHeaderTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user send HTTP requests with headers split across multiple TCP packets to exploit a zero Http.ReadHeaderTimeout, holding connections open and consuming server resources until 30%+ of node-processing capacity is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user exploit an unvalidated MaxTransactionsInBlock value of zero to cause the server to return empty blocks, preventing transaction data from being routed to 30%+ of network nodes that depend on block data for processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.Host] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unencrypted DB connection (sslmode=disable) to inject a DNS poisoning attack that redirects Db.Host to an attacker-controlled server, causing the mirror node to receive corrupted node data and route 30%+ of transactions to invalid nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeMap's use as the sole source of node routing (when non-empty) to cause a scenario where all configured nodes are in the same availability zone, and a targeted network partition removes 30%+ of them simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a NodeRefreshInterval that is shorter than the time required for the address book to propagate, causing the SDK to repeatedly fetch an incomplete node list and transiently remove 30%+ of nodes from its routing table?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxLifetime] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very short Pool.MaxLifetime to cause DB connections to expire mid-query, causing node-related queries to fail and retry, creating a thundering herd that overwhelms the DB and starves 30%+ of node-processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a zero ShutdownTimeout to cause the server to immediately terminate all in-flight requests during shutdown, aborting transactions destined for 30%+ of network nodes without allowing them to complete?
